description: Approved visual reference fixtures for stronger page hierarchy, denser enterprise workflows, and richer interaction states. Fixtures may be metadata-only until image assets are imported into the repository. Trust tiers help models prefer canonical product references over looser inspiration.
usageRules: Use visual fixtures after selecting a layout recipe and before freeform refinement.
Prefer higher-trust tiers first: product-canonical, then product-adjacent, then generic-inspiration.
Treat incomplete-placeholder fixtures as negative or low-confidence references unless the task explicitly needs a placeholder state.
Treat these fixtures as hierarchy and composition references, not pixel-perfect implementation targets.
Preserve UDS component usage and token rules even when borrowing visual rhythm from a fixture.
Prefer fixtures that match brand, route intent, and interaction state before borrowing from generic inspiration.
When image assets are unavailable, use the metadata as a structural and visual brief.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider account and communication settings
mustNotMiss: Centered identity summary above the settings content.
Distinct surfaced settings groups with editing affordances.
Tabbed profile subsections.
Bottom-aligned primary save action.
notes: More operational than the work-preferences flow.
Keep content readable with large surfaced modules.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider application work preferences
mustNotMiss: Strong identity header above the content tabs.
Soft grouped form panels rather than plain stacked fields.
Persistent primary save action.
Sidebar-driven workflow context.
notes: Calm guided workflow rather than dense analytics.
Use soft brand-tinted surfaces to group related questions.

fixtures.routeIntent: assignment requests grid with persistent filter panel
mustNotMiss: Status chips above the result grid.
Persistent right filter rail with stacked controlled filters.
Card-based request summaries with footer counts and actions.
Pagination controls anchored below the grid.
notes: Use for browse-and-filter request list pages.

fixtures.routeIntent: calendar month view with minimal clutter and no active overlays
mustNotMiss: Clean month grid with only active event bars.
Shell nav remains visible but secondary controls are minimized.
Top action row still includes export and add schedule.
Visual emphasis stays on the grid, not the filters.
notes: Use when the prompt asks for a cleaner calendar state.

fixtures.routeIntent: calendar month shell with shell nav and legend filters
mustNotMiss: Month switcher and today control above the calendar grid.
Legend toggles for unfilled, confirmed, and external states.
Month/week/day view toggle near the top right.
Export and add schedule actions aligned to the top bar.
notes: Use as the base calendar shell for connect.

fixtures.routeIntent: calendar month view with persistent filter rail
mustNotMiss: Left filter rail remains open beside the calendar.
Search and client selection appear above stacked filter controls.
Month grid stays visible with scheduled bars even when filters are open.
Apply and clear actions remain anchored in the filter rail.
notes: Use for search-and-refine calendar workflows.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider detail modal launched from calendar
mustNotMiss: Large provider name title in the modal header.
Detail block with NPI, specialty, phone, and email.
Assignment block with a visible status pill and view calendar action.
Calendar remains dimmed behind the modal.
notes: Use for provider-specific calendar drill-ins.

fixtures.routeIntent: schedule manager modal selecting unfilled shifts
mustNotMiss: Assignment summary card at top with fill progress.
Bulk select row before the shift checklist.
Shift list rendered as selectable rows with dates.
Primary management action stays near the top assignment summary.
notes: Use when users must select multiple shifts for action.

fixtures.routeIntent: week schedule modal with grouped unfilled and confirmed shifts
mustNotMiss: Week schedule title with total shift count.
Grouped lists by shift state such as unfilled and confirmed.
Each shift card shows request id, client, specialty, and time span.
Status pill aligned to each shift row.
notes: Use for modal summaries of a week bucket in the calendar.

fixtures.routeIntent: compact client status summary card
mustNotMiss: Client or hospital name as card title.
Three short status rows with right-aligned values.
Risk pill embedded in one of the rows.
Simple footer link to details.
notes: Use for compact client summary blocks inside dashboards or side panels.

fixtures.routeIntent: dashboard side panel focused on provider documents
mustNotMiss: Provider summary repeats at top of side panel.
Presentation and confirmation document groups are separated by headings.
View and download actions are inline with each document item.
Main dashboard remains visible underneath the right rail.
notes: Use when opening documents from provider cards on the dashboard.

fixtures.routeIntent: operational provider marketplace dashboard
mustNotMiss: KPI cards across the top with alert emphasis.
A larger surfaced work queue below KPIs.
Provider cards with status chips and primary actions.
Shell framing and footer context.
notes: Balance high-level metrics with actionable records.
Avoid pure card grids without a larger focal region.

fixtures.routeIntent: connect dashboard with KPI row, provider cards, client status, and embedded mini calendar
mustNotMiss: Wide KPI row with multiple alert-colored cards.
Since last visit summary band directly below KPIs.
Provider cards and client status list in parallel columns.
Embedded calendar with important dates panel on the right.
notes: Use for the most information-dense connect dashboard state.

fixtures.routeIntent: dashboard with provider details side panel open
mustNotMiss: KPI row and activity/dashboard cards remain visible in the main content.
Persistent right rail opens with provider details and document sections.
Document and travel sections are navigable rows inside the side panel.
Status and credential chips sit near the provider identity block.
notes: Use for dashboard drill-in flows that should not leave context.

fixtures.routeIntent: date range selection with prior-year comparison
mustNotMiss: Quick-select presets at the top.
Two visible calendars side by side.
Compare-to-prior-year surfaced section.
Selected date range summary and clear primary action.
notes: Use for reporting flows that need explicit compare-period logic rather than a simple picker.

fixtures.routeIntent: reduced filter drawer for simpler reporting modes
mustNotMiss: Only essential fields retained.
Generous empty space rather than filler controls.
Primary and secondary actions still anchored at bottom.
Date range remains central to the workflow.
notes: Use when the reporting view has fewer slicing dimensions.

fixtures.routeIntent: default reporting filter drawer
mustNotMiss: Explicit active filter count.
Saved preset affordance in the header.
Stacked filter groups with clear labels.
Primary apply and secondary clear actions at the bottom.
notes: A good default state for reporting filter drawers.

fixtures.routeIntent: filter drawer with search and multi-select list
mustNotMiss: Expanded multiselect section with search.
Scrollable result list with checkboxes.
Selections remain visible as chips in the field.
Primary actions remain anchored even in long drawers.
notes: Use for large option sets like worksites, providers, or specialties.

fixtures.routeIntent: priority filter drawer with expanded client picker and search
mustNotMiss: Expanded client section with inline search and long checkbox list.
Rest of the filter form remains visible below the expanded section.
Priority section remains at top even when additional filters expand.
Scrollbar or clear long-list affordance visible for large client sets.
notes: Use when the client selector is the primary refinement control.

fixtures.routeIntent: priority filters drawer with stacked additional filters
mustNotMiss: Priority filter group separated from additional filters.
Search, filter, and sort controls in drawer header.
Long stack of business filters below the priority group.
Primary apply and secondary clear actions anchored at the bottom.
notes: Use for request and provider priority triage filters.

fixtures.routeIntent: priority filters focused on provider workflow and credential risk
mustNotMiss: Priority filters name provider risk conditions directly.
Additional filters include provider types, states, worksites, and specialties.
Header retains result count and close/back affordances.
Actions remain unchanged even as priority meanings shift.
notes: Use for provider ops and credentialing review flows.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider details complete stage with finalized assignment records
mustNotMiss: Same card structure as on-assignment, but stage tab emphasizes completion.
Footer links are minimal and task-specific.
Provider list remains available at left for stage-wide browsing.
Do not overcomplicate this state with unnecessary actions.
notes: Use for completed assignment history inside provider details.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider details confirmed stage with credential chips and summary cards
mustNotMiss: Credential chips sit directly beneath each provider name.
Each provider card shows contact and request metadata in a three-column layout.
Light filter dropdown appears above the card list for substatus.
Documents and presentation summary links stay in card footers.
notes: Use when showing confirmed providers awaiting later stages.

fixtures.routeIntent: documents modal launched from provider details workspace
mustNotMiss: Provider identity remains repeated in the modal body.
Presentation and confirmation document groups are split clearly.
View and download links align per document row.
Background provider workspace remains visibly dimmed.
notes: Reuse the same document modal pattern across tracker and provider detail contexts.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider details on-assignment stage with simpler records
mustNotMiss: Provider cards simplify to dates, contacts, and request metadata.
Travel details and documents remain available in footer links.
No overuse of credential chips if the state is about active assignments.
Sort control remains above the list.
notes: Use for in-flight assignment tracking.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider details workspace focused on request details and upload area
mustNotMiss: Advanced controls area near the top of the detail content.
Long-form request details sections separated by headings.
Document upload drop zone at the bottom.
Provider details shell remains unchanged while inner content changes.
notes: Use for request metadata and upload-heavy states in the provider workspace.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider details shell with left provider list and right stage tabs
mustNotMiss: Persistent left list of providers with summary metadata.
Top right primary CTA for submit new request.
Stage tabs across the top of the detail region.
Active filter chips above the provider list.
notes: Use as the base provider workspace shell before selecting a stage variant.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider details workspace with left filter drawer open
mustNotMiss: Filter rail occupies the left side instead of the provider card list.
Priority filters above additional filters.
Right detail region keeps the stage tabs and selected provider content.
The same page shell works with and without the filter rail.
notes: Use when provider-stage pages need filtering without losing context.

fixtures.routeIntent: dark-mode current days summary analytics
mustNotMiss: Single wide hero chart with a compact summary rail.
Paired ranked tables below the chart.
Top tabs and filter row preserved across dark mode.
Readable legends and contrast against dark backgrounds.
notes: Pairs well with spend-summary dark fixture for multi-tab analytics suites.

fixtures.routeIntent: dark-mode spend summary analytics
mustNotMiss: Dark shell with bright high-contrast chart region.
Primary summary chart occupying the hero analysis band.
Supporting ranked panels below the hero chart.
Sparse but strong accent usage.
notes: This fixture is for dark-mode analytics only.
Do not simply invert colors; preserve surface hierarchy.

fixtures.routeIntent: reporting overview with persistent filter workspace
mustNotMiss: A dedicated side filter workspace, not just inline chips.
Active filter summary at top of drawer.
Date controls and presets in the same workflow.
Main reporting content remains readable behind the filter rail.
notes: Use when reporting prompts mention presets, saved filters, or complex slicing.
This fixture should bias AI toward workflow-oriented filtering.

fixtures.routeIntent: year-to-date reporting overview
mustNotMiss: Breadcrumb and report tabs above the analysis region.
Filter/date control row between tabs and content.
KPI band before the denser chart region.
Clear export affordance.
notes: This is the baseline reporting summary fixture for connect.
Use it before inventing ad hoc analytics compositions.

fixtures.routeIntent: future days report with grouped totals
mustNotMiss: Grouped rows by month or period.
Persistent filter and date controls above the table.
Financial total row at the bottom.
Negative values visibly distinguished.
notes: Use when reporting resolves to records, exports, and totals rather than charts.

fixtures.routeIntent: assignment request contact assignments by facility and approval role
mustNotMiss: Top-level request title with back action and save action.
Representative assignment panel above role-specific contact cards.
Two-column card grid for role ownership by facility.
Inline email contact details directly under selectors.
notes: Use when a workflow assigns named contacts to operational responsibilities.

fixtures.routeIntent: assignment request coverage details and staffing form
mustNotMiss: Header row with breadcrumb, title, and save/submit actions.
Secondary metadata links like bids, comments, contacts, and history near the title.
Tabbed request sections above the form body.
Large surfaced grouped field sections with legend-style labels.
notes: Use for request authoring and detail maintenance screens.

fixtures.routeIntent: documents modal launched from request tracker
mustNotMiss: Large modal title with provider name context.
Presentation and confirmation document sections separated by surfaced blocks.
View and download actions repeated for each document.
Provider/date summary visible above the document groups.
notes: Use for document preview and download states from tracker or provider detail pages.

fixtures.routeIntent: request tracker with left provider list and right detail region
mustNotMiss: Persistent left rail of provider summaries with search, chips, and filter action.
Top-level stage tabs for presentations, confirmed, on-assignment, complete, jobs, scheduler, request details, and calendar.
Right panel changes state by selected stage but preserves header and stage tabs.
Comparison controls or interest actions on presentation rows when relevant.
notes: Canonical split-workspace for request tracker flows.

fixtures.routeIntent: presentation summary modal from tracker workflow
mustNotMiss: Provider identity and date range repeated below the modal title.
Highlights section in a surfaced panel.
Availability and credential summary in a separate surfaced panel.
No unnecessary extra actions beyond close unless explicitly requested.
notes: Use for modal summaries that condense a provider presentation into readable bullets.

fixtures.routeIntent: request tracker cards comparing provider presentation states
mustNotMiss: Compact stacked provider cards with hospital and specialty context.
Small status pills on each card.
Vertical detail divider for request metadata.
Optional progress bar and document chips for incomplete profiles.
notes: Use for compact mobile-style provider tracker cards and comparison variants.

fixtures.routeIntent: requests and confirmations dashboard with activity lists
mustNotMiss: Large analytics or activity hero at top left.
Stacked request activity panel on the right.
Lower row of surfaced activity blocks with tabs or status counts.
Emphasis on recent operational events rather than only KPIs.
notes: This is a denser activity dashboard than the simpler KPI-first overview.

fixtures.routeIntent: campaign builder with performance list on left and form builder on right
mustNotMiss: Left column blends a compact analytics card with an item list.
Right column is a large structured form with grouped fields and rules.
Both columns are equally important and scrollable work regions.
Dense enterprise form controls without visual heaviness.
notes: Use for creation workflows where monitoring and editing coexist.

fixtures.routeIntent: campaign analytics dashboard with KPI row and campaign cards
mustNotMiss: Revenue headline paired with a colored numeric emphasis.
Small KPI strip above a grid of recent campaign cards.
Left nav uses grouped favorites and main menu sections.
Ambient background visuals add polish without reducing readability.
notes: Useful when dashboards need lighter marketing energy rather than strict enterprise density.

fixtures.routeIntent: course or learning dashboard with hero banner and mentor side rail
mustNotMiss: Large colorful hero banner near the top of the main content.
Horizontal learning progress cards under the hero.
Right rail with statistics and mentor/contact list.
Dense but cheerful card treatment.
notes: Useful for brighter dashboard treatments with a more consumer-like tone.

fixtures.routeIntent: deal detail workspace with activity column and task list
mustNotMiss: Narrow navigation on the left with active deal section highlighted.
Top identity row for the contact or deal owner.
Center content split between activity stream and current task list.
Lightweight detail tabs below the identity header.
notes: Useful for person-or-deal detail screens that need activity plus tasks in one frame.

fixtures.routeIntent: deal or workflow pipeline board with swimlanes and summary metrics
mustNotMiss: Summary metrics above the board.
Vertical columns with card stacks representing pipeline stages.
Media-rich cards with lightweight metadata and comments/files counts.
A soft ambient background that still leaves the board readable.
notes: Use when the prompt asks for a polished pipeline or board-based workflow.

fixtures.routeIntent: payments analytics overview with embedded query prompt and KPI side rail
mustNotMiss: Large payments or revenue chart occupying the main hero panel.
Inline exploration or prompt bar embedded in the hero surface.
Secondary metric cards and insight widgets supporting the hero.
Clean top nav and date filters.
notes: Useful when the product should feel analytical and assistive rather than purely operational.

fixtures.routeIntent: property operations summary with activity feed and map
mustNotMiss: Editorial or scenic visual context framing the dashboard.
Top KPI band for monthly stats.
Activity feed and recent requests stacked beside a map surface.
A mix of soft imagery and strict operational modules.
notes: Use when a product should feel more branded or atmospheric without losing utility.

fixtures.routeIntent: portfolio analytics dashboard with many chart bands
mustNotMiss: A long horizontal trend chart as the primary hero.
Small multiples or chart cards below for secondary analysis.
Left rail navigation kept visually quiet.
A highly data-dense surface with minimal decoration.
notes: Use for serious analytics surfaces where charts matter more than cards.

fixtures.routeIntent: risk operations dashboard with strong cards and vivid accent surfaces
mustNotMiss: Distinct color-block cards for risk and status summaries.
Large primary chart occupying the bottom-right hero area.
Sidebar and profile details feel integrated, not bolted on.
High-contrast accent use limited to key surfaces.
notes: Use when the product should feel bold and modern rather than neutral enterprise.

fixtures.routeIntent: storage and upload workspace with modular utility cards
mustNotMiss: Independent rounded cards with generous whitespace between modules.
Upload card centered as a primary task area.
Small analytics, contact, and file list cards acting as supporting utilities.
Playful but restrained illustration or media moments.
notes: This is a good inspiration source for lighter-weight utility dashboards.

fixtures.routeIntent: task productivity dashboard with schedule and notes
mustNotMiss: Friendly greeting with compact KPI strip.
Project table near the top of the workspace.
Schedule and notes cards paired below.
Lightweight left nav with project grouping.
notes: Useful when you want a softer, more personal dashboard tone.

fixtures.routeIntent: team productivity dashboard with performance chart and chat side panel
mustNotMiss: Large central performance chart with task list below.
Profile and messaging/activity side panel on the right.
Current tasks rendered as a clean operational table.
Soft neutral styling with subtle accent colors.
notes: Use when collaboration and personal productivity need equal emphasis.

fixtures.routeIntent: clinical operations overview with quick filters, KPI row, and appointment table
mustNotMiss: Quick filter band above the main reporting content.
KPI cards in a single scanning row.
Large surfaced operational table as the primary body section.
Brand-aware shell framing with compact left navigation.
notes: Use for operational overview pages that mix filters, metrics, and dense tabular content.

fixtures.routeIntent: team credentialing dashboard with central modules and right summary rail
mustNotMiss: Primary action strip near the top of the main workspace.
Stacked operational modules in the main column.
Persistent right-side identity and summary rail.
Module cards that surface onboarding, verification, expirables, and activity.
notes: Strong canonical reference for Modio dashboard composition and density.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider directory with chip filters, bulk checkbox selection, and table actions
mustNotMiss: Search and utility controls directly above the data table.
Filter chips visible before the table body.
Dense enterprise table with checkbox selection and row metadata.
Clear toolbar row for table-level actions.
notes: Use for provider directories and high-volume back-office table workflows.

fixtures.routeIntent: reporting workspace with tabbed categories, stacked coverage sections, and structured table panels
mustNotMiss: Tabbed reporting categories at the top of the content area.
Filter chip row below search controls.
Multiple stacked data panels for different reporting categories.
Panel-level utility actions that repeat consistently.
notes: Canonical Modio reporting coverage reference.

fixtures.routeIntent: reporting table with persistent bulk action bar after row selection
mustNotMiss: Bulk action toolbar appears only after selection.
Selected count is explicit in the toolbar.
Selected rows remain visually distinct in the table body.
Primary bulk action is visually dominant.
notes: Use for any enterprise workspace that supports row selection and batch processing.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider reporting detail with central reporting tables and persistent metadata side rail
mustNotMiss: Persistent right-side metadata rail for the selected provider.
Filter chip bar above reporting sections.
Multiple reporting panels stacked in the central workspace.
Primary provider identity region remains stable across tabs.
notes: Strong reference for Modio list-detail reporting and right-rail composition.

fixtures.routeIntent: facility dashboard with activity feed, due-date rail, and credentialing modules
mustNotMiss: Main column uses stacked operational modules and activity feed.
Right rail contains calendar and approaching due dates.
Top search and utility controls remain lightweight.
Dashboard modules use clear status indicators and progress states.
notes: Useful for facility and coordinator dashboards that need a calendar-backed side rail.

fixtures.routeIntent: unfinished placeholder frame with unresolved component instance slots
mustNotMiss: Large empty region with explicit unresolved instance-slot markers.
No product-grade content, hierarchy, or data density.
Use only as a negative or incomplete-state reference.
notes: Do not use this as a positive visual target.
Useful only to classify unfinished mock states or anti-pattern placeholders.

fixtures.routeIntent: job board availability step with persistent left results rail and guided form content
mustNotMiss: Persistent job result rail remains visible on the left.
The right pane is a guided single-step workflow, not a dense dashboard.
The form area uses large whitespace and one clear primary action.
Side navigation establishes product context throughout the flow.
notes: Use for CompHealth job board flows that progressively collect work preferences.
The main value is the split rail plus guided-step composition.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider detail workspace with central records tables and persistent right identity rail
mustNotMiss: A stable provider identity rail remains visible on the right.
The central workspace stacks multiple grouped record sections.
Chip-style filters sit above the section stack.
Each section behaves like an independently surfaced data block.
notes: Use this for Modio provider details pages focused on structured records rather than dashboard summaries.

fixtures.routeIntent: provider reporting workspace showing active row selection within a grouped reporting table
mustNotMiss: The selected row is visually highlighted inside the reporting table.
The provider identity rail remains stable and prominent.
Filter chips remain visible above reporting sections.
notes: Use as an adjacent state reference when row selection matters inside Modio reporting.

fixtures.routeIntent: team dashboard with coordinator profile rail, tracking calendar, and operational modules
mustNotMiss: Main dashboard modules occupy the center column.
Right rail emphasizes coordinator identity and due-date tracking.
Calendar and due-date cards are stacked in the rail.
notes: Use as an adjacent Modio dashboard state when the right rail is person-centric rather than provider-centric.

trustTierDefinitions.product-canonical: Best available representation of a real UDS product workflow or page state. Prefer these first for generation and repair.
trustTierDefinitions.product-adjacent: Close to a real UDS product pattern but either partial, sparse, or not yet the strongest canonical reference for the flow.
trustTierDefinitions.generic-inspiration: Cross-industry inspiration for density, hierarchy, and polish. Use only after product-canonical references are considered.
trustTierDefinitions.incomplete-placeholder: Low-confidence placeholder or slot reference. Use only when the goal is to represent an unfinished or empty integration state.
